pericopes.pericopeTitle: THE GLORY ENTERS THE TEMPLE, AND THE MESSAGE IS GIVEN

entries.author: St. Jerome
entries.title: BEHOLDING GOD RE-CREATES US.
entries.body: When his face is revealed and we contemplate the glory of God, we are reformed in the image of the Creator. {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Jerome
entries.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST.
entries.body: The earth shone with his glory, which really takes place at the coming of Christ, when the sound of the apostles goes forth on the whole earth and their words to the ends of the earth. {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Jerome
entries.title: THE VOICE SPEAKS TO AN OVERWHELMED PROPHET.
entries.body: Immediately the Spirit raised me up, for I myself was lying and not able to go any further, and he led me to the inner courtyard, for I had fallen outside. And behold, I who had earlier glimpsed the glory of the God of Israel coming from the east, now saw the house of the Lord filled with his glory, and I heard a voice speaking as someone speaking to me from inside the house. {fn:0}

entries.author: Theodoret of Cyr
entries.title: THE MAN IS LIKE THE ARCHANGEL MICHAEL.
entries.body: He showed me that there was an angel who measured the building, and he showed me these things; and he was very like Michael, who was given the responsibility of looking after the people. {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Jerome
entries.title: THE EUCHARISTIC TABLE.
entries.body: There is a wall between me and them, so that a very short wall divides the sacrifices of the priests and the place for the mystery of the body and blood of Christ. {fn:0}

footnotes.text: CCL 75:626. An allusion to church architecture of the time.

entries.author: St. Jerome
entries.title: THE CHURCH LASTS FOREVER.
entries.body: He did not dwell for a short time as in the synagogue, but forever, as is shown in the church of Christ. {fn:0}

entries.author: St. Jerome
entries.title: THE CHURCH OF CHRIST.
entries.body: For the house and the law of all the teachings of God and the city that is built on top of the mountain are to be believed from what is written; a city on a hill cannot be hid, and there is a river whose streams make glad the city of God,{fn:0} which clearly refers to the church of God. {fn:1}
